name: Carrot-Raisin Muffins
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Wheat Germ, toasted        1/8 teaspoon Nutmeg
1 1/2 teaspoons Baking Powder             1 1/3 cups Shredded Carrots
1 teaspoon Baking Soda                     1/2 cup Raisins (soaked in hot water)
2 tablespoons Brown Sugar Substitute       2 Tablespoons Vegetable Oil
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Spray muffin tins with nonstick vegetable spray or line with paper muffin cups.
Combine dry ingredients in a large bowl: flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
Stir in toasted wheat germ.
In a separate bowl, beat eggs and oil together.
Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Gently fold in shredded carrots and soaked raisins.
Divide batter evenly among 12 muffin cups.
Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Nutritional Information:
1 muffin: 105 calories, 1 gram protein, 4 grams total fat, 16 grams carbohydrates, 1 gram fiber, 213 mg sodium, 118 mg potassium, 46 mg cholesterol.
